headline_summary: Hormuz shipping remains constrained as major operators fail to complete transits despite Iranian assurances.
Conflict spillover hits Gulf infrastructure: drone attacks damage Kuwait airport radar and missile-intercept debris sparks fires in Abu Dhabi.
Iran-linked Houthis launch first attack on Israel in this war, raising risk of renewed Red Sea disruption.
Industrial assets in the UAE report significant damage from Iranian strikes, widening economic exposure.
US policy signals price-management focus via temporary sanctions waiver allowing Iranian oil sales at sea.
risk_drivers: Strait of Hormuz transit unreliability / shipping paralysis
Expansion of strikes to Gulf airports/ports/industrial zones
New front opened by Houthis (Yemen) with potential Red Sea spillover
Unclear degradation of Iran's remaining missile/drone inventory
Policy uncertainty (US operational escalation vs. price-management tools)

Energy: Upside tail-risk for Brent due to persistent Hormuz transit failures; tanker availability and insurance constraints remain binding.
Red Sea risk could reintroduce container and product shipping delays, widening energy logistics premia.
FX: Risk-off episodes favor USD/CHF/JPY; EM oil importers face renewed pressure if energy prices stay elevated.
Gulf asset sentiment sensitive to infrastructure hits despite pegs; watch reserve/liquidity signals.
Rates: Higher energy-driven inflation risk keeps breakevens supported; risk-off can steepen credit spreads.
Event risk may increase demand for duration hedges even as supply shocks complicate policy expectations.
Equities: Gulf equities show sensitivity to escalation headlines; global equities likely rotate toward energy/defense while transport suffers.
Higher volatility regime persists while conflict expands to additional theatres and infrastructure.

sources.title: Most Gulf markets ease on fears of broader Iran conflict
Yemen's Houthis confirm launching attack on Israel for first time in current war
Chinese ships halt attempt to exit Hormuz despite Iran safe passage assurances
Kuwait international airport suffers 'significant' radar system damage after drone attacks
Emirates Global Aluminium reports 'significant damage' from Iranian strikes

events.title: Iranian missile and drone attacks damage Emirates Global Aluminium Al Taweelah base
events.implication: Shows conflict spillover into UAE industrial assets; raises regional risk premium and supply-chain disruption beyond energy.

events.title: Two Chinese COSCO container ships abort first Gulf exit attempt via Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Demonstrates safe-passage assurances are not restoring traffic; prolongs effective shipping paralysis and supports oil/LNG risk premia.

events.title: Drone attacks damage radar system at Kuwait International Airport, authorities say
events.implication: Highlights widening target set to Gulf aviation and critical infrastructure; increases operational disruption and insurance costs.

events.title: Debris from missile interception near KEZAD sparks fires and injures five in Abu Dhabi
events.implication: Even successful intercepts create domestic disruption; reinforces escalation risk for Gulf hubs and ports.

events.title: Yemen's Houthis confirm launching first missile attack on Israel in current war
events.implication: Marks Houthi entry into the Iran-linked conflict; raises probability of renewed Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb shipping attacks.

events.title: US issues 30-day waiver allowing sale/delivery of Iranian-origin oil loaded at sea
events.implication: Signals US willingness to use sanctions flexibility to manage oil prices; may partially offset supply shock but underscores severity of disruption.

events.title: Reuters: US can only confirm about a third of Iran's missile arsenal destroyed
events.implication: Suggests Iran retains meaningful strike capacity; supports tail-risk of prolonged conflict and further infrastructure hits.

events.title: Gulf stock markets slip as Houthis enter conflict; Brent ends Friday up sharply
events.implication: Shows market transmission channel: equities risk-off and elevated energy prices feeding broader risk premia.

links.from: Two Chinese COSCO container ships abort first Gulf exit attempt via Strait of Hormuz
links.why: Abortive transit underscores that traffic remains constrained, sustaining oil risk premium.

links.from: Yemen's Houthis confirm launching first missile attack on Israel in current war
links.why: Additional front increases uncertainty and tends to trigger risk-off positioning.

links.from: Yemen's Houthis confirm launching first missile attack on Israel in current war
links.why: Houthi entry raises chance of Red Sea/Bab el-Mandeb attacks affecting energy-related shipping.

links.from: Drone attacks damage radar system at Kuwait International Airport, authorities say
links.why: Wider targeting in Gulf can pressure regional assets and EM sentiment.

links.from: US issues 30-day waiver allowing sale/delivery of Iranian-origin oil loaded at sea
links.why: Sanctions flexibility can add barrels, partially offsetting disruption-driven price pressures.

links.from: Reuters: US can only confirm about a third of Iran's missile arsenal destroyed
links.why: Remaining capability implies longer conflict risk, supporting wider spreads and volatility.
